differentials.name: circumferential (longitudinal) type I collagen bundles parallel to long axis of meniscus → hoop strength resisting axial load + preventing meniscal extrusion
differentials.raw: circumferential (longitudinal) type I collagen bundles parallel to long axis of meniscus → hoop strength resisting axial load + preventing meniscal extrusion

differentials.name: radial thin fibers perpendicular to longitudinal bundles forming a lattice → tying bundles together + providing structural support

differentials.name: Transverse meniscal (geniculate) ligament (83-90%) =  thin fibrous band that connects and stabilizes the anterior horns of the menisci

differentials.raw: Meniscofemoral ligaments (MFL) in 89-93% (a)  Wrisberg ligament

differentials.name: Popliteomeniscal fascicles (visualized in 90%) (a)  anteroinferior fascicle = floor of popliteal hiatus (b)  posterosuperior fascicle = roof of popliteal hiatus =  synovial-lined fibrous bands that attach to posterior horn of lateral meniscus + help form popliteal hiatus
differentials.raw: Popliteomeniscal fascicles (visualized in 90%) (a)  anteroinferior fascicle = floor of popliteal hiatus (b)  posterosuperior fascicle = roof of popliteal hiatus =  synovial-lined fibrous bands that attach to posterior horn of lateral meniscus + help form popliteal hiatus

differentials.name: Popliteal hiatus =  separates lateral meniscus from joint capsule
differentials.raw: Popliteal hiatus =  separates lateral meniscus from joint capsule

differentials.name: Oblique meniscomeniscal ligament (1-4%) =  connects meniscal horns in X-wise fashion on AXIAL image (a)  medial oblique meniscomeniscal ligament

differentials.name: Diskoid meniscus =  abnormally shaped enlarged meniscus with further central extension onto tibial articular surface
differentials.raw: Diskoid meniscus =  abnormally shaped enlarged meniscus with further central extension onto tibial articular surface

differentials.name: Meniscal flounce =  rippled appearance of free nonanchored inner edge of medial meniscus

differentials.raw: Meniscal ossicle (rare)

context: =  quadriceps muscle consisting of Insertion:  combined as quadriceps tendon on patella Pes Anserinus [pes, Latin = foot; anser, Latin = goose] =  tendinous configuration of 3 flexors + medial rotators of knee joint attaching inferomedially to tibial tuberosity mnemonic:   Say GraceSe before eating goose =  high-riding patella as normal anatomic variant Cause:   patellar tendon too long → reduced patellar contact area → patellar dislocation ◊  High-riding patella in 25% of patients with acute patellar dislocation MR measurement: patellar height ratio (Insall-Salvati index) = TL÷PL = patellar t
